items.d: you have this when your head hurts
items.s: My head hurts. I have got a bad ___.
items.hintDe: Das hast du, wenn dein Kopf wehtut.

items.d: you have this when one of your teeth hurts
items.s: One of my teeth hurts, so I have got ___.
items.hintDe: Das hast du, wenn dir ein Zahn wehtut.

items.d: you have this when your ear hurts
items.s: My ear hurts, so I have got ___.
items.hintDe: Das hast du, wenn dir das Ohr wehtut.

items.d: you have this when you eat too much and the middle of you hurts
items.s: Jacob has eaten too much cake, so now he has ___.
items.hintDe: Das hast du oft, wenn du zu viel gegessen hast.
items.note: d avoids the headword's tokens 'stomach'/'ache'; describes the body location instead.

items.d: you have this when the bottom of your leg, just over your foot, hurts
items.s: Emily cannot stand on her foot. She has got ___.
items.hintDe: Das hast du, wenn dir der Knöchel über dem Fuß wehtut.

items.d: the middle of your leg that you go down on
items.s: Jacob has hurt his ___ in the middle of his leg, so he cannot play football.
items.hintDe: Das ist in der Mitte deines Beins.

items.d: you have this when your back hurts
items.s: My back hurts when I stand up. I have got ___.
items.hintDe: Das hast du, wenn dir der Rücken wehtut.

items.d: the inside place that food and drink go down after your mouth
items.s: My ___ hurts when I drink cold milk.
items.hintDe: Da gehen Essen und Trinken hinunter.

items.d: you wash in this when you are dirty
items.s: I have to give my dog a ___. She is very dirty.
items.hintDe: Darin wäschst du dich, wenn du schmutzig bist.

items.d: you have this when you are ill and it makes you well again
items.s: The doctor gives me ___ when I am ill.
items.hintDe: Das nimmst du, wenn du krank bist, damit es dir besser geht.
items.note: masterlist sentence 'you should take your medicine' uses untaught take/should; reworded with taught words, sense preserved.

items.d: what you keep in your head, so you can bring days and people back
items.s: Grandma has a very good ___. She can keep lots of birthdays in her head.
items.hintDe: Damit behältst du Namen und Tage im Kopf.
items.note: masterlist 'remember lots of facts' uses untaught remember/facts; reworded, sense preserved.

items.d: somebody who is ill and that a doctor looks after
items.s: The doctor looks after every ___ at the hospital.
items.hintDe: So nennt man einen Kranken, wenn er beim Arzt ist.

items.d: you eat your soup with this, not with a knife
items.s: I need a ___ to eat my soup.
items.hintDe: Damit isst du deine Suppe.

items.d: this red wet thing is inside you
items.s: There is ___ on your shirt. It is all red.
items.hintDe: Das Rote, das aus dir kommt, wenn du dich verletzt.

items.d: a tall thing in the street with a light on it
items.s: He has just run into a ___ in the street.
items.hintDe: So ein hohes Ding an der Straße mit einem Licht oben.
items.note: masterlist 'walked into a lamp post' uses untaught walked; SB present-perfect frame with taught 'run' used instead.

items.d: a thing that makes you well again when you are ill
items.s: In Ancient Egypt, a dead mouse was a ___ for toothache.
items.hintDe: Etwas, das einen kranken Menschen wieder gesund macht.

items.d: to make somebody well again when they are ill
items.s: Long ago, people said worms could ___ stomach ache.
items.hintDe: Einen kranken Menschen wieder gesund machen.

items.d: the doctor who looks after your teeth
items.s: I want to become a ___.
items.hintDe: Der Arzt, der sich um deine Zähne kümmert.

items.d: to push food down so it is not in pieces
items.s: They ___ a dead mouse and mixed it with food.
items.hintDe: Essen so weich drücken, dass keine Stücke mehr da sind.
items.note: Carrier is the SB 'Medicine from the past' text verbatim; blank takes the past form 'mashed' (parallel with 'mixed').

items.d: to put a food and a drink into one so they become one thing
items.s: I ___ the medicine into the food, so the dog eats it.
items.hintDe: Zwei Dinge zu einem zusammenrühren.
items.note: masterlist 'I mix my pet's medicine with its food' uses genitive 'pet's' (untaught token); reworded to 'the dog'.

items.d: what a food or drink is like in your mouth, good or bad
items.s: The ___ was very good.
items.hintDe: Wie etwas im Mund schmeckt.

items.d: you clean your teeth with this every morning and night
items.s: In Ancient Rome, people did not have ___.
items.hintDe: Damit putzt du dir die Zähne.

items.d: a long thing with no legs that lives in the ground
items.s: A ___ lives under the ground.
items.hintDe: So ein langes Tier ohne Beine, das im Boden lebt.

items.d: what your nose finds; bad fish has a really bad one
items.s: The ___ of fish was really bad.
items.hintDe: Das, was du mit der Nase wahrnimmst.

items.d: the help you give somebody who is hurt, before the doctor is there
items.s: We have ___ classes at school.
items.hintDe: Die schnelle Hilfe für Verletzte, bevor der Arzt da ist.

items.d: always happy to help people
items.s: She has always been a great and ___ friend.
items.hintDe: Wenn jemand gerne anderen hilft.

items.d: very bad and not nice at all
items.s: The accident was ___!
items.hintDe: Sehr schlimm, ganz und gar nicht schön.

items.d: a child at school
items.s: Every ___ in our class does a text in front of the teacher.
items.hintDe: Ein Kind, das in die Schule geht.

items.d: from a time in the past up to now
items.s: I have been in hospital ___ last week.
items.hintDe: Von einem Zeitpunkt in der Vergangenheit bis jetzt.
items.note: since is a function word; mc are other non-noun bank words. Difficulty 3: abstract, no concrete referent.

items.d: you said this when you want people to think a thing is true
items.s: It is true! ___ I am not telling a lie.
items.hintDe: Das sagst du, wenn andere dir glauben sollen.
items.note: Idiom: blank takes whole phrase. Distractors are other in-bank fixed phrases (all gate-clean), none a variant of the answer.

items.d: to hurt your arm, leg or knee in an accident
items.s: I do not want to ___ my knee again.
items.hintDe: Sich einen Körperteil wehtun, zum Beispiel bei einem Unfall.
items.note: 'hurt' is a defensible near-synonym in the sentence, so it is a partial answer (the cued word is 'injure').

items.d: you said this to tell somebody a thing is not bad
items.s: I have lost your pen. - ___ I have one more for you.
items.hintDe: Das sagst du, wenn etwas kein Problem ist.
items.note: Idiom from STORY OF THE STONES everyday-English box. Distractors are other in-bank fixed phrases (all gate-clean).

items.d: somebody who makes books and stories for people to read
items.s: My favourite ___ has a new book out this week.
items.hintDe: Jemand, der Bücher oder Geschichten schreibt.
